title: Iterative Testing Loops
concept: Running test suites against generated code and requiring the agent to modify code until all tests pass.

exercises.prompt: What is important for debugging code effectively?

options.text: Providing specific line numbers and error definitions
Using random guesses to fix errors

exercises.explain: Providing specific line numbers and error definitions helps pinpoint where problems are in the code.

cards.title: What are Iterative Testing Loops?
cards.body: Iterative testing loops are cycles where code is tested, modified, and retested until it works correctly.

cards.title: Definition of Iterative Testing Loops
cards.body: It is a process where an agent runs tests on code, fixes issues, then runs the tests again until all tests pass.

cards.title: How Testing is Like a Puzzle
cards.body: Think of testing code like solving a puzzle. You try pieces (tests) and if they don't fit (fail), you adjust and try again!

cards.title: Example of an Iterative Testing Loop
cards.body: Imagine an agent creates a function to add numbers. It tests the function, sees it fails to add correctly, modifies it, and tests again until it works.

cards.title: Myth: Testing is a One-Time Task
cards.body: Testing is not just done once; it's an ongoing process of improvement until the code works as expected!

cards.title: Flow of Iterative Testing
cards.body: Testing, Modifying, and Retesting is key.

left.text: All tests are successful! Keep going!

exercises.prompt: What is the primary goal of running test suites against generated code?

options.text: To improve code until it passes all tests
To write code faster without checking
To make the code look pretty

exercises.explain: The primary goal is to improve the code until it passes all tests, ensuring it works correctly.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Iterative Testing Loops involve making changes to code even if it previously passed tests.
exercises.explain: False: Iterative Testing Loops focus on modifying code when tests fail, not when they pass.

exercises.prompt: Complete the sentence: Iterative Testing Loops are processes of _____, modifying, and retesting until the code works correctly.
exercises.sentence: Iterative Testing Loops are processes of testing, modifying, and retesting until the code works correctly.
exercises.explain: The correct word is 'testing', as it reflects the primary activity in these loops.

exercises.prompt: Which option correctly describes the iterative testing process?

options.text: Run tests, ignore failures, and finalize code.
Test code, modify it if needed, and retest.
Create code without any tests involved.

exercises.explain: The correct description is that you test code, modify it if needed, and then retest, which captures the iterative nature.

exercises.prompt: What is the correct order of steps in an Iterative Testing Loop?

options.text: Test, finalize code, and ignore errors.

exercises.explain: The correct order is to first test the code, then modify it if necessary, and retest. This ensures each version of the code is verified before moving on.
